codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of FunASR transcription service with VAD, ASR, and punctuation restoration. The code is well-structured with clear separation between model management, pipeline execution, segment building, and persistence. 37 unit tests provide thorough coverage of the core logic with mocked models, plus 2 integration tests for GPU-based verification. A few non-blocking observations noted below.

issues.description: persist_transcript() hardcodes memo status to 'completed' and transcript_revision=1. The job lifecycle feature (m3-job-lifecycle) also sets transcript_revision=1 on completion (jobs.py:513). If persist_transcript is called as part of the job pipeline, the revision will be set to 1 here, then overwritten to 1 again by the job completion handler. Not a bug since values match, but the dual ownership of this state is worth noting for future maintenance — if the job service starts incrementing revisions differently, persist_transcript could silently override.

run_punc() silently swallows all exceptions and returns the original text. While this is intentional for resilience, it means punctuation restoration failures are completely invisible to callers. The logger.warning at line 204 is good, but there's no way for the transcription pipeline to report that punctuation was skipped. Consider adding a flag in the result dict indicating whether punc restoration succeeded.

transcribe() auto-calls self.load() at line 228, but run_vad/run_asr/run_punc raise TranscriptionError('Models not loaded') if called individually without loading. This inconsistency means the public API behaves differently depending on entry point: transcribe() is auto-loading but the individual methods are not. This is fine for current usage (transcribe is the main entry point) but could surprise future callers.

The _SENTENCE_END set includes semicolon (;) as a sentence-ending character. Semicolons are not typically sentence boundaries in either Chinese or English — they connect related clauses. This could produce unexpectedly short segments for text containing semicolons. The Chinese semicolon (；) is not included, creating an asymmetry.

_build_segments_from_vad() uses character-count proportional splitting (chars_per_ms), which doesn't respect word or sentence boundaries. For Chinese this works fine (character-based language), but for English or other space-delimited languages, this could split words mid-way. This is only a fallback path (when no token timestamps are available), so impact is limited.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created .factory/library/funasr-integration.md documenting FunASR API quirks (remote_code path requirement, inference_with_vad bug, timestamp format, openai-whisper dependency). This is good — it captures hard-won integration knowledge that would otherwise be lost.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/funasr-integration.md — covers model loading quirks, output format, segment building strategy, environment variables

sharedStateObservations.observation: The transcription service uses a module-level singleton pattern (get_models() / _models global) which is consistent with the architecture's single-worker design but not documented as a pattern in AGENTS.md. Other services (like the database) use dependency injection instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/app/services/transcription.py:490-507 — get_models() and is_model_ready() use a module-level _models singleton. Compare to app/db.py which uses get_connection() with DI-style patterns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: persist_transcript() directly imports and uses get_connection() from app.db, taking db_path as a parameter. Other parts of the codebase use dependency injection for database connections. This is a minor inconsistency in database access patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/app/services/transcription.py:423 — 'from app.db import get_connection'; line 446: 'conn = get_connection(db_path)'

sharedStateObservations.observation: FunASR and openai-whisper are installed in the Docker container but not listed in pyproject.toml. The worker documented this as intentional (too large for pip install during development), but this means the dependency graph is incomplete for anyone reading pyproject.toml alone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff notes: 'FunASR and openai-whisper are installed in the container venv but not in pyproject.toml. They are too large for pip install during normal development and should be pre-installed in the base Docker image.'

summary: The m3-funasr-integration feature is well-implemented and passes review. The transcription service correctly integrates FunASR AutoModel with separate VAD/ASR/Punc model loading (avoiding the known inference_with_vad bug), builds segments from token-level timestamps with VAD fallback, persists raw and final transcript JSON files plus SQLite segments, and supports hotwords. 37 unit tests thoroughly cover segment building logic, model lifecycle (mocked), and persistence. The implementation properly defers SSE event streaming and job worker loop integration to the m3-job-lifecycle feature as designed. A few non-blocking issues noted: semicolon included in sentence-end set, proportional VAD text splitting doesn't respect word boundaries for English, and persist_transcript() has overlapping memo status ownership with the job service. Shared state observations capture useful FunASR integration knowledge that the worker documented in .factory/library/.
